notes: FT057 covers the full live-voice social-engineering TTP; F1040 is the closest F3 anchor but only describes the caller-ID spoofing tradecraft commonly used to support vishing, so the relationship is partial.
No F3 / MITRE ATT&CK technique covers the ClickFix fake-CAPTCHA + Win+R + user-pasted PowerShell pattern; closest neighbors T1204 User Execution and T1660 Phishing are too generic.

ft3_name: Mobile Banking Trojan Distribution and Device Takeover
notes: FT057 covers the full mobile banking trojan distribution and on-device takeover TTP (sideloaded recruitment-themed loaders, HVNC, web-injects, keylogging, MaaS economics); T1453 is the closest F3 anchor but only captures the accessibility-abuse subcomponent, and T1219 Remote Access Tools is adjacent — no single F3 technique describes mobile banking trojan distribution-as-a-technique.

ft3_name: Phishing: Real-Time OTP Relay via Operator-Controlled Phishing Kit
notes: FT3 narrows T1111 to fraud-platform OTP relay during real-time fraudulent payment / ATO authorization driven by an operator console; F3 T1111 is the broader MFA-interception umbrella with no current FT3 counterpart.

ft3_name: Falsifying Business Documents: Live-Source Tax Certificate Forgery
notes: FT026.006 is a live-data-forgery refinement of the FT026 / F1027 family: F1027 covers falsified business documents broadly, but does not carve out the live-source-of-truth variant where data accuracy is preserved and only the seal / issuance authority is forged.
